name: Mechanism stabilising the EW vacuum against the SM near-criticality
closability_note: Near-criticality may be coincidence, anthropic, or structural. Could dissolve experimentally — current top-mass uncertainty straddles the stability boundary.
summary: The SM Higgs quartic runs to a small negative value near M_Pl, leaving the EW vacuum metastable but extremely long-lived. No framework predicts why we sit so close to the stability boundary.
what_closes_it: A mechanism that flattens lambda(M_Pl) via new high-scale physics (positive beta_lambda contributions), ties near-criticality to inflation (critical-Higgs / Bezrukov-Shaposhnikov), or supplies anthropic selection. SUSY is excluded because it removes the metastability entirely.
scale_hint: Planck scale (10^19 GeV). The stability boundary is set by lambda(M_Pl); the closing mechanism must operate at or near M_Pl. Note that susy_scalar_partners is excluded because SUSY removes the metastability puzzle entirely — squark/slepton contributions keep lambda positive up to M_Pl, but at the cost of decoupling from this gap.

principles_at_stake.note: Near-criticality is itself either a fine-tuning or a structural prediction; the closing mechanism's stance on naturalness determines which.

literature_anchors.citation: Buttazzo, Degrassi, Giardino, Giudice, Sala, Salvio, Strumia, 'Investigating the near-criticality of the Higgs boson', JHEP 12 (2013) 089
Bezrukov and Shaposhnikov, 'The Standard Model Higgs boson as the inflaton', PLB 659, 703 (2008)
Bednyakov, Kniehl, Pikelner, Veretin, 'Stability of the electroweak vacuum: gauge independence and advanced precision', PRL 115, 201802 (2015)
Khoury and Steinhardt, 'Coincident inflation and the Higgs metastability puzzle' (2019)
